title: Anchorage Digital and Binance Launch Off-Exchange Settlement for Institutional Crypto Trading
summary: Anchorage Digital and Binance have launched an off-exchange settlement service, allowing institutions to trade on Binance while keeping their assets in segregated custody at Anchorage Digital Bank instead of on the exchange. This reduces counterparty risk and enhances security for large-scale crypto trading.
body:
Anchorage Digital and Binance have introduced a new off-exchange settlement service tailored for institutional crypto traders. This service allows institutions to execute trades on Binance's platform while maintaining custody of their assets at Anchorage Digital Bank in segregated accounts, rather than holding them on the exchange itself.

The collaboration aims to address the security concerns of institutional investors by keeping their assets in segregated custody. This setup reduces counterparty risk, as institutions no longer need to transfer their assets to Binance for trading purposes. The service is designed to provide a secure and efficient trading environment for large-scale investors.

This development is significant for institutional investors who have been cautious about the security risks associated with holding assets on exchanges. By leveraging Anchorage Digital's custody services, institutions can trade on Binance with greater confidence, knowing their assets are held separately and securely. This could attract more institutional capital into the crypto market, potentially increasing liquidity and market stability.

For those interested in institutional crypto trading, this partnership offers a robust solution for secure and efficient trading. Institutions should consider this service if they are looking to mitigate counterparty risk while accessing Binance's trading platform. Keep an eye on further developments in this space as more institutions adopt similar practices.
